# Beudox HR - Technical Context

## Overview
Beudox HR is a React-based HR management system using Supabase as the backend. It's built with modern web technologies and follows a component-driven architecture.

## Architecture

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build Tool**: Vite with SWC for fast development
- **Routing**: React Router DOM with protected routes
- **State Management**: 
  - React Query for server state (API calls, caching)
  - React Context for authentication state
  - Local component state for UI interactions
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens
- **Component Library**: shadcn/ui built on Radix UI primitives

### Backend Integration
- **Database**: Supabase PostgreSQL
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with RLS policies
- **API**: Direct Supabase client calls (no custom API layer)
- **Real-time**: Not currently implemented

### Key Design Patterns
- **Role-Based Access Control**: Features shown/hidden based on user roles (hr_manager, finance_manager, ceo)
- **Layout Composition**: AppLayout wraps protected pages with sidebar and topbar
- **Query Keys**: Consistent naming for React Query cache invalidation
- **Component Variants**: UI components support multiple visual variants

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Core Application Files
- `src/main.tsx`: Application entry point, renders App component
- `src/App.tsx`: Main app component with routing and providers
- `src/index.css`: Global styles, font imports, CSS custom properties

### Authentication
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Auth context provider, manages session and employee data
- `src/pages/Login.tsx`: Login form with Supabase auth
- `src/pages/ForgotPassword.tsx`: Password reset request
- `src/pages/SetPassword.tsx`: Password update for invites/recovery

### Layout Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout wrapper
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Collapsible navigation sidebar
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Page header with title
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variants

### Pages
- `src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`: Main dashboard with stats cards
- `src/pages/NotFound.tsx`: 404 error page

### Utilities
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Database type definitions

### UI Components
- `src/components/ui/`: shadcn/ui components (buttons, forms, dialogs, etc.)
- `src/hooks/use-toast.ts`: Toast notification system

## Database Schema (Key Tables)
- `companies`: Company information
- `employees`: Employee records with auth_user_id
- `employee_roles`: Junction table for roles
- `roles`: Available roles (hr_manager, finance_manager, ceo)
- `attendance_imports`: Monthly attendance data
- `payroll_records`: Payroll processing records
- `projects`: Project management

## Business Logic
- **Authentication Flow**: Login → Fetch employee data → Redirect to dashboard
- **Role Permissions**: Dashboard shows different stats based on user role
- **Data Fetching**: React Query handles loading states and error handling
- **Navigation**: Sidebar items defined in config, active state based on current route

## Development Workflow
- **Development Server**: `npm run dev` (Vite on port 8080)
- **Building**: `npm run build` (production build)
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit tests
- **Linting**: ESLint with React and TypeScript rules
- **Type Checking**: TypeScript compiler

## Configuration Files
- `vite.config.ts`: Build configuration with React plugin and path aliases
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind configuration with custom colors and fonts
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `eslint.config.js`: ESLint configuration
- `package.json`: Dependencies and scripts

## Environment Variables
- `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`: Supabase project URL
- `VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`: Public Supabase key
- `VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID`: Project identifier

## Current Implementation Status
This is Sprint A0 (Foundation) - implements authentication, basic layout, and dashboard with role-based stats. Additional features (employee management, attendance, payroll) are planned for future sprints.
